what_it_does: This file is a structured design audit and pattern inventory of a 16-slide investor/partner pitch deck for ConceptV / Project Vi. It records the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual grammar, color/type/spacing rules, reusable components, and content-budget samples extracted from the deck.
observations: The file catalogues two alternating visual modes: immersive chrome (full-bleed 3D architectural render with sparse UI overlay) and content chrome (warm near-white background with blueprint watermark).
It provides a slide-by-slide attention_flow list describing how the eye is expected to move across each of the 16 pages.

Findings are grouped into categories such as chrome-mode, gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, line-connector, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, zone, sequence, texture, and attention-mechanism.
Each finding includes a recipe, evidence slide references, an emergent flag, and a generator_rule for reproduction.

A components section defines reusable UI blocks: stat highlight box, golden arrow bullet list, pill button row, icon-to-hub diagram, product distribution tree, person card, product badges, traction stat row, compare column layout, V brand badge, and contact card overlay.
Content samples specify character/word/line budgets for H1, H2, H3, bullets, stats, pill labels, italic descriptors, person fields, and contact-card fields.
The color system is tightly constrained: warm cream background, near-black warm text, a single brand gold accent, and white reserved only for UI chrome/contact cards.
Geometry is semantically assigned: hexagon = Property Wizard hub, diamond = Vi front-end, circle = people/integration hub, rounded rectangle = containers/pills/data boxes.
Spacing follows an 8px modular scale with explicit vertical rhythm rules.
The deck is framed by immersive bookend slides (p-01 cover, p-16 close) that surround the business argument with product-experience proof.
standouts: Immersive bookend grammar: only p-01 and p-16 use full-bleed architectural renders with sparse UI chrome.
Contrast pair device: p-02 and p-03 split dark complexity (network graph / screenshot collage) on the left against light structured clarity on the right, linked by a golden arrow.
Pill-row summary seal: a 4-5 pill button row appears as a claim closer on product and strategy slides.
Golden numeral as visual anchor: data is consistently rendered with oversized golden numbers followed by smaller grey labels.
Hexagon/diamond badge system: two distinct shapes encode the two products (Property Wizard vs Vi) and appear as floating territory markers over screenshot grids.
conventions: Uses a consistent JSON schema with category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent boolean, and generator_rule for every finding.
Content slides default to a warm near-white background (#F7F5F0 to #F5F2EB) with a faint blueprint watermark at 4-7% opacity.
All bullets use a golden triangle marker and are constrained to a single line.
Spacing is expressed as multiples of an 8px base unit.
Split layouts consistently place the visual/diagram on the left and structured text on the right.
The V brand badge (golden circle with white V) is required in the bottom-right corner of every content slide.
The brand gold (#C9A227) is the only color accent; no additional colors are introduced.
H1 titles are near-black, H2 sub-headings are brand gold, and body text is warm dark tone.
Rounded pill buttons never wrap text and always share the same height within a row.
Dashed lines signal integration/planned/future connections, while solid lines signal current relationships or transitions.
concerns: Some measurements are approximate and deck-scale dependent (e.g., 'approximately 70-80px at deck scale'), which may not translate directly to other canvases.
The content_samples entry for 'stat-label-2line' gives 'Virtual Hubs' as an example, but that text is one word/one line while the slot claims 2 lines.
Several findings are marked emergent=true despite appearing to be deliberate design choices, which could blur the distinction between intentional and accidental patterns.
No source image files, checksums, or extraction methodology are included, so provenance and reproducibility are unclear.
The generator rules are highly prescriptive and may be brittle if the deck content or aspect ratio changes.
notes: A dense, prescriptive design-system snapshot of a single pitch deck, useful as a reproduction rulebook but needing provenance and scale normalization before fusion.
questions: What is the exact source image resolution and aspect ratio for the 16 slides?
Was the attention_flow derived manually, from eye-tracking data, or inferred by the extractor?
Which tool or process produced this extracted JSON?
Are the font families confirmed (Poppins/Nunito) or estimated?
Do the slide images themselves exist in the repository for verification?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe the 15-page/middle-compression pitch deck; the actual content is a comprehensive design-system pattern library and generator rulebook derived from that deck, going well beyond simple description.
apparent_themes: Visual grammar as a formal, reproducible system
Mono-accent premium warmth and architectural materiality
Before/after contrast as a spatial argument device
Product experience as emotional proof surrounding business logic
Systematic reuse of components and spatial patterns
Strict content budgeting and single-line discipline
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to turn a finished pitch deck into a machine-readable design language: encoding layout, typography, color, motion of attention, and reusable components so the ConceptV deck style can be replicated, extended, or generated.
novelty: It treats a pitch deck as a structured grammar with explicit attention-flow choreography, spatial argument devices (dark/left chaos → light/right clarity), and semantic geometry (hexagon/diamond/circle) rather than as a loose collection of slides.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, slide-level inventory of visual patterns, component specifications, content budgets, and attention-flow heuristics that a fused design system can reference when generating or auditing pitch-deck layouts in the ConceptV style.
summary_for_embedding: A detailed design-system audit and pattern rulebook for the ConceptV/Project Vi 2026 pitch deck, cataloging slide grammar, chrome modes, geometry, typography, color, components, attention flow, and content budgets.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the visual and narrative design language of a 16-slide investor pitch deck so it can be reproduced, extended, or fused into a generative design system.
